title: Giant-tree and deep-pocket phase
summary: Forty-second structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-3 wave turns the strongest forest vertical family into more destination-like exploration by adding one giant-tree continuation that feels special to climb and one deeper cave pocket that feels secret but recoverable.

execution_order.why_now: One climb that feels special will add more wonder than another generic height increase.
Lane 3 is ready to turn the canopy family from coherent into memorable.
The climb should stay readable and recoverable while still feeling more special.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the deeper cave pocket follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: Once the climb is strong, the cave side should gain one more hidden place without becoming a harsher network.
A single secret-feeling chamber is the next best way to deepen wonder on the cave side.
The new depth should feel magical and fair, not simply lower or harsher.

guardrails: Keep the spaces cozy and recoverable.
Reuse the current tiny cue language instead of inventing a new one.
Avoid widening into a large cave network or traversal shell.
Redwood-like scale cheats are acceptable when they improve wonder and play.
acceptance_focus: One giant-tree climb feels like a real destination instead of just another ledge chain.
One deeper cave pocket feels hidden and recoverable rather than punishing.
Lane 3 stays wonder-focused without adding combat or a denser traversal HUD.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-giant-tree-and-deep-pocket-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 3's next vertical wave: add one giant-tree destination climb and one deeper cave pocket while keeping the spaces cozy, readable, and free of combat or HUD bloat.
